stages.name: Ingest and segment 10-K filing
stages.prompt_template:
Segment this 10-K filing into its standard item sections.

{{filing_text}}

stages.name: Extract financial statement line items
stages.prompt_template:
Extract income statement, balance sheet, and cash flow line items as JSON from the filing sections below.

{{ingest_filing.output}}

stages.name: Extract risk factor disclosures
stages.prompt_template:
List and categorize the risk factors disclosed in Item 1A.

{{ingest_filing.output}}

stages.name: Normalize financials to standard schema
stages.prompt_template:
Normalize these extracted line items to our standard valuation schema (revenue, cogs, opex, ebitda, net_income, total_assets, total_liabilities, operating_cash_flow, capex).

{{extract_financials.output}}

stages.name: Compute valuation ratios
stages.prompt_template:
Compute EV/EBITDA-relevant ratios (gross margin, EBITDA margin, net margin, current ratio, debt/equity, FCF margin) from these normalized financials.

{{normalize_financials.output}}

stages.name: Compare ratios against peer set
Score disclosed risk severity
stages.prompt_template:
Score each disclosed risk factor's severity (1-5) and likelihood (1-5) for a valuation discount.

{{extract_risk_factors.output}}

stages.name: Score management tone sentiment
stages.prompt_template:
Rate management's tone in this commentary as bullish / neutral / cautious, with a one-sentence justification.

{{extract_management_commentary.output}}

stages.name: Synthesize valuation adjustment factors
Generate valuation memo narrative
stages.prompt_template:
Write a valuation memo narrative (3-4 paragraphs) for an investment committee based on these adjustment factors.

{{synthesize_valuation.output}}

stages.name: QA check memo against source financials

query.filing_text:
NORTHWIND ROBOTICS, INC. — FORM 10-K (FY2025)
Item 1A. Risk Factors: Dependence on a small number of automotive OEM customers; exposure to semiconductor component shortages; unionization risk at the Ohio plant.
Item 7. MD&A: Revenue grew 22% YoY driven by expansion into warehouse automation. Management remains confident in the FY2026 order backlog despite supply constraints.
Financial Statements: Revenue $412M, COGS $247M, Opex $98M, Net Income $41M, Total Assets $520M, Total Liabilities $210M, Operating Cash Flow $58M, Capex $34M.
query.peer_set: Industrial automation peers: median EBITDA margin 18%, median gross margin 42%, median D/E 0.6

1. Customer concentration — severity 4, likelihood 3.

input.sentiment_analysis.output: cautious — management cites confidence in backlog but explicitly flags ongoing supply constraints as an offsetting concern.

records.output: Multiple contraction of ~0.5x recommended given below-peer margins and elevated supply-chain risk (severity 3, likelihood 4). Discount rate adjustment: +50bps for customer concentration risk. Partially offset by above-average revenue growth (22% YoY), which supports a modest terminal-growth premium.

input.synthesize_valuation.output: Multiple contraction of ~0.5x recommended given below-peer margins and elevated supply-chain risk (severity 3, likelihood 4). Discount rate adjustment: +50bps for customer concentration risk. Partially offset by above-average revenue growth (22% YoY), which supports a modest terminal-growth premium.

records.output:
## Valuation Memo — Northwind Robotics, Inc.

Northwind Robotics posted 22% YoY revenue growth to $412M, driven by expansion into warehouse automation, but profitability metrics trail peers: EBITDA margin of 16.3% sits below the 18% sector median, and gross margin of 40% is two points below peers at 42%.

Risk disclosures center on automotive OEM customer concentration and semiconductor supply shortages, the latter scored as both severe and likely to persist through FY2026. Management's tone is cautiously confident -- backlog strength is cited alongside explicit supply-chain caveats.

We recommend a ~0.5x multiple contraction versus sector peers and a 50bps discount rate premium for customer concentration, partially offset by above-average top-line growth supporting a modest terminal-growth premium.

input.generate_narrative.output:
## Valuation Memo — Northwind Robotics, Inc.

Northwind Robotics posted 22% YoY revenue growth to $412M, driven by expansion into warehouse automation, but profitability metrics trail peers: EBITDA margin of 16.3% sits below the 18% sector median, and gross margin of 40% is two points below peers at 42%.

Risk disclosures center on automotive OEM customer concentration and semiconductor supply shortages, the latter scored as both severe and likely to persist through FY2026. Management's tone is cautiously confident -- backlog strength is cited alongside explicit supply-chain caveats.

We recommend a ~0.5x multiple contraction versus sector peers and a 50bps discount rate premium for customer concentration, partially offset by above-average top-line growth supporting a modest terminal-growth premium.

records.output: No discrepancies found. All figures in the memo (revenue, EBITDA margin, gross margin, YoY growth) match the normalized financials and computed ratios.

query.filing_text:
CASCADE BIOLOGICS, INC. — FORM 10-K (FY2025)
Item 1A. Risk Factors: Lead candidate CBL-104 in Phase III trials, no guarantee of FDA approval; reliance on a single contract manufacturer; going-concern dependent on additional financing within 18 months.
Item 7. MD&A: No commercial revenue yet; R&D spend increased 31% YoY to advance CBL-104 Phase III readout expected Q2 FY2026. Cash runway estimated at 14 months.
Financial Statements: Revenue $6M (licensing), COGS $1M, Opex $88M, Net Income -$76M, Total Assets $140M, Total Liabilities $45M, Operating Cash Flow -$71M, Capex $4M.
query.peer_set: Mid-cap biotech peers: median EBITDA margin -5% (pre-commercial), median gross margin 68%, median D/E 0.3

input.sentiment_analysis.output: cautious — management is forward-looking about the Phase III catalyst but explicitly discloses a 14-month cash runway, an implicit near-term financing overhang.

records.output: Given binary regulatory risk (severity 5) and going-concern disclosure (severity 5, likelihood 3), apply a substantial discount rate premium (+400bps) reflecting elevated cost of capital pre-catalyst. Valuation should be treated as option-like on the Phase III outcome rather than multiple-based; peer EBITDA-margin comparison is not meaningful pre-commercial.

input.synthesize_valuation.output: Given binary regulatory risk (severity 5) and going-concern disclosure (severity 5, likelihood 3), apply a substantial discount rate premium (+400bps) reflecting elevated cost of capital pre-catalyst. Valuation should be treated as option-like on the Phase III outcome rather than multiple-based; peer EBITDA-margin comparison is not meaningful pre-commercial.

records.output:
## Valuation Memo — Cascade BioLogics, Inc.

Cascade BioLogics remains pre-commercial, with $6M in licensing revenue against $88M of opex driven primarily by a 31% YoY increase in R&D spend ahead of the CBL-104 Phase III readout expected Q2 FY2026. Net loss for the period was $76M, and management discloses an estimated 14-month cash runway.

Two disclosed risks dominate the valuation picture: Phase III / FDA approval uncertainty (severity 5, likelihood 3) and going-concern risk tied to financing needs within 18 months (severity 5, likelihood 3). A single contract manufacturer dependency adds moderate supply-chain risk.

Given the binary regulatory catalyst and near-term financing overhang, we recommend valuing Cascade option-like on the Phase III outcome rather than applying a peer EBITDA multiple, and layering a +400bps discount rate premium to reflect elevated cost of capital pre-catalyst.

input.generate_narrative.output:
## Valuation Memo — Cascade BioLogics, Inc.

Cascade BioLogics remains pre-commercial, with $6M in licensing revenue against $88M of opex driven primarily by a 31% YoY increase in R&D spend ahead of the CBL-104 Phase III readout expected Q2 FY2026. Net loss for the period was $76M, and management discloses an estimated 14-month cash runway.

Two disclosed risks dominate the valuation picture: Phase III / FDA approval uncertainty (severity 5, likelihood 3) and going-concern risk tied to financing needs within 18 months (severity 5, likelihood 3). A single contract manufacturer dependency adds moderate supply-chain risk.

Given the binary regulatory catalyst and near-term financing overhang, we recommend valuing Cascade option-like on the Phase III outcome rather than applying a peer EBITDA multiple, and layering a +400bps discount rate premium to reflect elevated cost of capital pre-catalyst.

records.output: No discrepancies found. Revenue, opex, net income, and cash-runway figures in the memo match the normalized financials and filing disclosures.
